phenomenon: From primordial nucleosynthesis to rocky planet formation
key_measurement: Big Bang nucleosynthesis (BBN) at T+3 min produced H ~75%, He-4 ~25% by mass, D/H ~2.527×10⁻⁵ (Cooke et al., 2018, ApJ 855); Planck 2018 baryon density Ω_b h² = 0.02237 ± 0.00015
institutions: University of California Santa Cruz (UCSC) — nucleosynthesis group

sources.citation: Planck Collaboration: Aghanim, N. et al. (2020) 'Planck 2018 results. VI. Cosmological parameters', Astronomy & Astrophysics 641:A6
Cooke, R.J., Pettini, M., Steidel, C.C. (2018) 'One Percent Determination of the Primordial Deuterium Abundance', Astrophysical Journal 855(2):102
Dauphas, N. & Chaussidon, M. (2011) 'A Perspective from Extinct Radionuclides on a Young Stellar Object: The Sun and Its Accretion Disk', Annual Review of Earth and Planetary Sciences 39:351–386

visualization_compliance: BBN timeline must place nucleosynthesis at T+100 s–T+20 min post-Big Bang — do not conflate with stellar nucleosynthesis epoch (Gyr later)
Cosmic timeline to rocky planet formation spans ~9 Gyr from Big Bang to Solar System formation at T+9.2 Gyr — label timestamps on all timeline frames
First-generation (Population III) stars must not be depicted with Earth-like rocky planets — heavy elements absent before first supernova enrichment
CMB recombination epoch (T ~380,000 yr, T_cmb ~3000 K) must be placed correctly on timeline if depicted
Primordial composition bar (H/He only) must not show metals — label 'Population III gas cloud: H + He only'
Label SCHEMATIC on universe evolution diagrams; do not present CMB maps as simulations without citing Planck data release
Rocky planet formation timescale (~10–50 Myr via planetesimal accretion) must be indicated on any protoplanetary disk sequence
